question_data.passage: What we typically call education is, on closer inspection, two quite different activities operating under a single name. The first is the transmission of established knowledge — historical facts, mathematical procedures, scientific findings, linguistic conventions. The second is the cultivation of capacities for independent thought — the ability to evaluate evidence, construct arguments, and arrive at conclusions one can defend. These two activities can support each other, but they can also work at cross-purposes. A curriculum designed primarily for transmission may produce students who can recite established knowledge while lacking the capacity to evaluate it critically. A curriculum focused entirely on independent reasoning may produce students with weak grasp of the foundational knowledge that meaningful inquiry requires. The most thoughtful educational traditions have sought to integrate both, but the integration is more difficult to achieve than either component alone.

questions.stem: 下線部 'A curriculum designed primarily for transmission may produce students who can recite established knowledge while lacking the capacity to evaluate it critically.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 主として知識の伝達を目的に設計されたカリキュラムは、確立された知識を暗唱することはできても、それを批判的に評価する能力を欠いた生徒を生み出すことがある。
questions.explanation: 【東大型】① A curriculum designed primarily for X = 主として X を目的に設計されたカリキュラム (過去分詞の後置修飾) ② may produce students who V = V する生徒を生み出すことがある (関係詞 who) ③ recite established knowledge = 確立された知識を暗唱する ④ while lacking X = X を欠きながら (分詞構文 while + V-ing) ⑤ the capacity to evaluate it critically = それを批判的に評価する能力。

question_data.passage: There is a striking pattern in how human societies generate and propagate beliefs. Beliefs that match what the believer's social environment rewards tend to spread, regardless of their accuracy. Beliefs that conflict with social rewards often face suppression, again regardless of their accuracy. The pattern raises uncomfortable questions about the relationship between truth and what large numbers of people believe — questions that few societies have addressed with sustained seriousness. The mechanism is not primarily about deliberate deception. People who hold beliefs aligned with social rewards usually believe them sincerely. The selection happens earlier, in which beliefs people are willing to entertain and which they reject without consideration. Truths that would impose social costs are not rejected after careful evaluation; they often go unconsidered entirely. The implication is that even widespread agreement on contested matters provides weaker evidence of truth than we typically assume.

questions.stem: 下線部 'Truths that would impose social costs are not rejected after careful evaluation; they often go unconsidered entirely.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 社会的な代償を課しかねない真理は、慎重な評価の末に拒絶されるのではなく、しばしば検討の俎上にすら上ることなく終わるのである。
questions.explanation: 【東大型】① Truths that would impose social costs = 社会的代償を課すことになる真理 (関係代名詞 + would 仮定的) ② not rejected after careful evaluation = 慎重な評価の末に拒絶されるのではなく ③ go unconsidered entirely = 検討されぬまま完全に終わる (go + 形容詞: 〜のままになる)。

question_data.passage: The most important things we know about the world are not, on the whole, the things we have figured out for ourselves; they are the things passed down to us by those who came before — discoveries we did not make, distinctions we did not draw, frameworks we did not invent — and the recognition of how much we owe to this inheritance is itself one of the marks of a thoughtful mind.

questions.stem: 上記英文を日本語に和訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 我々が世界について知っている最も重要な事柄は、概して、自ら見出したものではなく、先人たちから受け継がれたものである。すなわち、我々が為したのではない発見、我々が引いたのではない区別、我々が考案したのではない枠組みである。そして、こうした遺産にどれほど負っているかを認識することそれ自体が、思慮深い精神の徴のひとつなのである。
questions.explanation: 【東大型】① The most important things we know about X = X について知る最も重要な事柄 (関係詞省略) ② not A; they are B 構造 ③ on the whole = 概して ④ the things we have figured out for ourselves = 自ら見出した事柄 ⑤ those who came before = 先人 ⑥ discoveries we did not make 等3並列の関係詞節 ⑦ the recognition of X is itself one of the marks of Y = X の認識それ自体が Y の徴の一つ。

question_data.passage: It is rarely the case that important problems are solved by the application of intelligence alone; far more often, solutions emerge from the combination of intelligence with the practical experience, accumulated trial and error, and tacit knowledge that those who have actually grappled with the problem have built up over years.

questions.stem: 上記英文を日本語に和訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 重要な問題が知性のみの適用によって解決されることは稀である。むしろ、解決ははるかに多くの場合、知性と、当該の問題に実際に取り組んできた者たちが何年もかけて積み上げてきた実践的経験、試行錯誤の蓄積、暗黙知との組み合わせから生まれるのである。
questions.explanation: 【東大型】① It is rarely the case that S V = S V することは稀である ② the application of intelligence alone = 知性のみの適用 ③ far more often, S V = はるかに多くの場合 S V する ④ emerge from the combination of A with B = A と B の組み合わせから生まれる ⑤ tacit knowledge = 暗黙知 ⑥ those who have actually grappled with X = X に実際に取り組んできた者たち ⑦ build up over years = 何年もかけて積み上げる。

question_data.passage: The technological transformations of the past two decades have been so rapid that thoughtful evaluation of their consequences has barely begun. Smartphones, social media, algorithmic recommendation systems, and now artificial intelligence have reshaped how we work, communicate, learn, and form beliefs — yet our understanding of these effects remains rudimentary. Some effects are clearly positive: information access has democratized in ways earlier generations could not have imagined. Some are clearly concerning: rates of anxiety and depression among adolescents have risen alongside smartphone adoption in patterns difficult to dismiss as coincidence. Many effects remain contested or simply unknown. The pattern reveals an uncomfortable truth: we have deployed technologies at vast scale before developing the tools to evaluate them. The bill for this premature deployment may not become fully visible for decades. The thoughtful response is neither uncritical embrace nor reactive rejection, but the slower work of building genuine understanding of what these technologies do — to individuals, to relationships, to institutions, to societies — alongside the willingness to revise our use of them as understanding develops.

questions.stem: 【東大型・要約 70-80字】上記英文の主旨を 70-80 字の日本語で要約せよ。
questions.answer: 近年の技術は評価より早く社会に浸透し、効果は未解明である。無批判な受容も反射的拒絶でもなく、影響の理解と用途修正を並行して進める姿勢が求められる。(78字)
questions.explanation: 【東大型・要約】① technological transformations 急速・評価未熟 ② some positive / some concerning / many unknown ③ deployed before evaluation tools ④ thoughtful response = neither embrace nor rejection but understanding + revise。要約では「評価不足の現実」と「対処姿勢」の2点に絞る。

questions.stem: 【東大型・自由英作 60-80 words】Some people argue that AI should never make decisions about human lives — for example, in courts or hospitals. Others say AI can sometimes make better decisions than humans. Discuss your view.
questions.answer: I believe AI should support but not replace human decisions about lives. AI can identify patterns humans miss and reduce certain biases, making it valuable as input to human judgment. However, AI systems lack moral responsibility, can encode existing biases at scale, and cannot account for context that responsible decisions require. The hybrid approach — AI providing analysis, humans making final decisions with accountability — captures benefits while preserving the moral agency that life-affecting decisions deserve. (76 words)
questions.explanation: 【東大型】Position 中道 + AI の長所 + AI の限界 + Hybrid 結論。60-80語厳守、論理構造 (Pos → Pro → Con → Synthesis)。

question_data.passage: There is a feature of human moral life that philosophers have long debated and recent psychology has illuminated in unexpected ways. We typically experience our moral judgments as conclusions reached through careful reasoning. We considered the relevant factors, weighed competing claims, and arrived at the position we hold. Yet psychological research suggests this picture often inverts the actual sequence. Moral conclusions frequently come first, generated by intuition operating below conscious awareness; reasoning then constructs justifications for the conclusions intuition has already reached. The implications of this finding deserve careful consideration. They do not mean that moral reasoning is worthless or that all moral conclusions are equally arbitrary. Some intuitions track features of situations that genuinely warrant moral attention; others do not. The challenge is distinguishing reliable from unreliable intuitions — a task that requires precisely the kind of careful examination that intuition alone cannot perform.

questions.stem: 下線部 'reasoning then constructs justifications for the conclusions intuition has already reached' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: その後、推論は、直観がすでに到達した結論に対する正当化を構築するのである。
questions.explanation: 【京大型】① reasoning constructs justifications for X = 推論は X への正当化を構築する ② the conclusions intuition has already reached = 直観がすでに到達した結論 (関係詞省略 + 過去完了)。京大は連鎖関係詞を頻出させる。

question_data.passage: The genuinely difficult moral questions are not those where the right answer is obvious and the only difficulty is summoning the courage to act on it; they are those where the right answer is itself unclear, where reasonable people examining the same evidence can reach different conclusions, and where the most we can do is to act with appropriate humility about the possibility of being wrong.

questions.stem: 上記英文を日本語に和訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 真に困難な道徳的問題とは、正答が明白でただそれを行動に移す勇気を奮い立たせることが難しいだけのものではない。それは、正答自体が不明瞭であり、同じ証拠を吟味する分別ある人々が異なる結論に至りうるような問題、そして我々にできる最善が、誤っているかもしれない可能性に対する適切な謙虚さをもって行動することだけであるような問題なのである。
questions.explanation: 【京大型】① The genuinely difficult moral questions = 真に困難な道徳的問題 ② not those where ... ; they are those where ... 構造 (二重関係副詞 where) ③ summon the courage to V = V する勇気を奮い立たせる ④ reasonable people examining the same evidence = 同じ証拠を吟味する分別ある人々 ⑤ the most we can do is to V = 我々にできる最善は V することだ ⑥ with appropriate humility about X = X に対する適切な謙虚さをもって。

questions.stem: 【京大型・自由英作 80-100 words】「教育は型を覚えることから始まるべきだ」という考えと、「教育は問いを立てることから始まるべきだ」という考えがある。どちらに賛成か、理由を 80-100 語で書け。
questions.answer: I believe education should begin with learning forms before progressing to questioning. While questioning is essential to mature thought, premature questioning without foundational knowledge often produces shallow inquiry. Students need vocabulary, factual frameworks, and worked examples before they can meaningfully evaluate ideas. The greatest mathematicians, writers, and thinkers all studied existing forms intensively before innovating. Learning forms first does not preclude later questioning — it provides the substrate that makes questioning productive rather than merely contrarian. The progression matters: forms first, then questions about those forms, then original contributions building on or departing from them. (94 words)
questions.explanation: 【京大型・自由英作】Position + 反論への譲歩 + 具体例 (mathematicians, writers, thinkers) + 進歩構造 (forms→questions→originality) + 結論。京大は「対比軸の精緻化」を評価。

question_data.passage: There is a feature of contemporary working life that researchers have begun to document with troubling consistency: the gap between what work is meant to be and what work has often become. Work, in its idealized form, is meant to provide both economic livelihood and meaningful engagement — using human capacities to produce value that benefits oneself and others. Yet much contemporary work involves tasks workers themselves describe as meaningless: filling out forms that no one reads, attending meetings that produce no decisions, generating reports that serve no purpose. The phenomenon, sometimes called 'bullshit jobs,' has provoked debate about whether such work serves any productive function or simply persists due to organizational inertia and the social need for adults to be employed.

questions.stem: 下線部 'the gap between what work is meant to be and what work has often become' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 仕事とはどうあるべきか、ということと、仕事がしばしばどのようになってきたか、ということとの間の隔たり
questions.explanation: 【阪大型】① the gap between A and B 構造 ② what work is meant to be = 仕事とはどうあるべきか (関係代名詞 what + be meant to be) ③ what work has often become = 仕事がしばしばどのようになってきたか (現在完了)。

questions.stem: 【阪大型・自由英作 100 words】「都市集中」と「地方分散」のどちらが日本社会にとって望ましいか、自分の意見を 100 語程度で述べよ。
questions.answer: I believe Japan should pursue greater regional balance rather than continued urban concentration. Excessive Tokyo-centric concentration creates several problems: housing prices that exclude middle-income families, regions facing population decline despite rich resources, and vulnerability when single-point concentration meets disasters. Distributed cities — strengthening Sapporo, Sendai, Hiroshima, Fukuoka — would preserve regional culture, reduce housing pressure, and increase national resilience. Remote work technology now enables this rebalancing without sacrificing economic productivity. While Tokyo will remain Japan's most important city, treating it as the only viable destination has become a liability rather than an advantage. (101 words)
questions.explanation: 【阪大型】Position + 4 specific benefits + 反論への組込 (Tokyo の役割は否定しない) + 結論。100語ぴったりで Band 高評価。

question_data.passage: In economic theory, markets coordinate decentralized choices through price signals, producing outcomes that no central planner could match. The theory has substantial empirical support — markets have indeed produced prosperity at scales unimaginable in centrally planned economies. Yet the theory works imperfectly in important domains. When transactions create costs or benefits for parties not part of the exchange — pollution, public health effects, climate impacts — market prices fail to capture these externalities. Information asymmetries between buyers and sellers can produce market failures. Some goods — basic research, public infrastructure, deep cultural transmission — markets systematically underprovide because individual consumers cannot capture the full benefits.

questions.stem: 下線部 'When transactions create costs or benefits for parties not part of the exchange ... market prices fail to capture these externalities.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 取引が、その交換の当事者ではない第三者にコストや便益を生じさせる場合、市場価格はそうした外部性を捉えそこなうのである。
questions.explanation: 【一橋型】① When transactions create X for Y = 取引が Y に X を生じさせる場合 ② parties not part of the exchange = 取引の当事者ではない者 (= 第三者) ③ fail to capture X = X を捉えそこなう ④ externalities = 外部性 (経済学用語)。

question_data.passage: The wealth of a society, measured purely in material terms, often masks the more important question of how that wealth is distributed and to what ends it is directed; a country that produces enormous economic output while leaving substantial portions of its population in precarious circumstances cannot reasonably claim success on the basis of aggregate numbers alone.

questions.stem: 上記英文を日本語に和訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 純粋に物質的な観点で測られた社会の豊かさは、その富がいかに分配され、いかなる目的に向けられているかという、より重要な問いを覆い隠すことがしばしばある。膨大な経済産出を生み出しながら、人口のかなりの部分を不安定な境遇に置いている国家は、集計値のみを根拠に成功を主張することは、合理的にはできないのである。
questions.explanation: 【一橋型】① wealth measured in material terms = 物質的観点で測られた豊かさ ② mask = 覆い隠す ③ how X is distributed and to what ends X is directed = X がいかに分配され、いかなる目的に向けられているか (二重間接疑問文) ④ a country that V = V する国 ⑤ leaving X in precarious circumstances = X を不安定な境遇に置く (分詞構文) ⑥ on the basis of X alone = X のみを根拠に。

question_data.passage: The relationship between scientific knowledge and democratic decision-making has become increasingly fraught. Science produces findings about complex matters — climate, public health, economic dynamics — whose policy implications affect everyone. Democratic theory holds that affected citizens should participate in decisions about their lives. Yet the technical complexity of relevant science often exceeds what most citizens can evaluate directly. The gap creates genuine tensions. Pure technocracy — letting experts decide while citizens accept their conclusions — undermines democratic accountability. Pure democracy — treating citizen opinions as equally valid regardless of relevant expertise — produces incoherent policy when scientific facts matter for outcomes. The thoughtful response acknowledges both pressures: maintaining democratic legitimacy while developing institutions that translate scientific knowledge into terms ordinary citizens can engage with productively.

questions.stem: 下線部 'Pure democracy — treating citizen opinions as equally valid regardless of relevant expertise — produces incoherent policy when scientific facts matter for outcomes.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 純粋な民主政、すなわち市民の意見を、関連する専門知識の有無に関わらず等しく妥当なものとして扱う民主政は、科学的事実が結果に影響する場合、首尾一貫しない政策を生み出すのである。
questions.explanation: 【名大型】① Pure democracy = 純粋な民主政 ② treating X as Y = X を Y として扱う (現在分詞による同格説明) ③ regardless of X = X に関わらず ④ relevant expertise = 関連する専門知識 ⑤ produces incoherent policy = 首尾一貫しない政策を生み出す ⑥ when scientific facts matter for outcomes = 科学的事実が結果に影響する場合。

question_data.passage: In any examination of how technologies shape human behavior, attention often focuses on dramatic features — the smartphone interface, the algorithmic recommendation, the artificial intelligence assistant. Yet much of technology's actual influence operates through subtler mechanisms that go largely unnoticed. The default settings of devices and applications shape behavior more than most users realize. The friction (or absence of friction) between actions affects which actions get taken. The feedback patterns built into systems train users in ways that conscious design choices often did not anticipate. These subtle features matter because they operate continuously across millions of interactions, accumulating effects that no single interaction would suggest.

questions.stem: 下線部 'These subtle features matter because they operate continuously across millions of interactions, accumulating effects that no single interaction would suggest.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: こうした微細な特徴が重要なのは、それらが数百万もの相互作用を通じて持続的に機能し、単一の相互作用からは想像もつかないような効果を蓄積していくからである。
questions.explanation: 【東工大型】① These subtle features matter because S V = こうした微細な特徴が重要なのは S V するからである ② operate continuously across X = X を通じて持続的に機能する ③ accumulating effects that V = V する効果を蓄積しながら (分詞構文) ④ no single interaction would suggest = 単一の相互作用からは想像もつかない (would 仮定的)。

question_data.passage: Among the more striking features of recent decades has been the dramatic shift in how knowledge work is organized. Where earlier generations of professionals typically worked for single employers across decades-long careers, contemporary knowledge workers increasingly move between organizations frequently, work as independent contractors, or combine multiple income streams from different sources. This shift has consequences that extend across personal finance, professional identity, and the relationships between workers and employers. Some observers celebrate the new flexibility, which allows workers to match their evolving interests with appropriate opportunities and reduces dependence on any single employer's fortunes. Others lament the erosion of stable employment relationships that supported planning, family formation, and career-long skill development. Most thoughtful analyses acknowledge that the shift produces both gains and losses, and that the distribution of these effects varies substantially across worker characteristics, industries, and economic circumstances.

questions.stem: What is the central shift the author identifies?
choices: Knowledge workers move between organizations more frequently, work as contractors, or combine income streams
questions.explanation: 段落1「move between organizations frequently, work as independent contractors, or combine multiple income streams」が根拠。

questions.stem: 【早稲田型・自由英作 100-120 words】Some argue that strict rules and clear boundaries are essential for raising responsible children. Others believe children develop best when given freedom and trusted to make their own choices. Express your view.
questions.answer: I believe both structure and freedom matter, but in different proportions at different ages. Young children benefit substantially from clear rules and predictable consequences — they lack the cognitive development to evaluate complex situations and need external structure while internal regulation develops. As children mature, however, the proportion should shift toward graduated freedom. Adolescents need opportunities to make meaningful choices and experience their consequences, including some that go badly, to develop the judgment that adulthood requires. Parents who maintain rigid control through adolescence often produce adults unable to function autonomously. The art is matching the balance to developmental stage rather than choosing one approach permanently. (108 words)
questions.explanation: 【早稲田型】Position + 発達段階による調整 + 具体例 (young vs adolescent) + 反例 (rigid control) + 結論。早稲田は「対立軸の精緻化」を評価。

question_data.passage: Among the more important developments in contemporary economic life has been the rise of what economists call the 'attention economy' — business models in which user attention becomes the primary commodity exchanged. Free social media platforms, search engines, and content aggregators do not charge users monetary fees; they instead capture attention that they sell to advertisers. The model has produced both genuine benefits and substantial concerns. Users gain access to powerful tools at no monetary cost. Advertisers reach precisely targeted audiences. Yet the underlying logic — that platforms succeed by maximizing user engagement regardless of whether that engagement produces user welfare — has produced documented harms. Engagement-maximizing algorithms tend to amplify content that produces strong emotional responses, often regardless of accuracy.

questions.stem: 下線部 'platforms succeed by maximizing user engagement regardless of whether that engagement produces user welfare' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: プラットフォームは、ユーザーのエンゲージメントが彼らの福利を生み出すかどうかに関わらず、そのエンゲージメントを最大化することによって成功するのである。
questions.explanation: 【慶應型】① platforms succeed by V-ing = プラットフォームは V することによって成功する ② maximizing user engagement = ユーザーのエンゲージメントを最大化する ③ regardless of whether S V = S V するかどうかに関わらず ④ user welfare = ユーザーの福利。

questions.stem: 【慶應型・自由英作 120-150 words】Some argue that universities should be evaluated primarily by graduate employment rates and starting salaries. Others say universities should be evaluated by less easily measured outcomes — civic engagement, cultural literacy, lifelong learning. Discuss your view.
questions.answer: I believe universities should be evaluated by both measurable and harder-to-measure outcomes, but with greater weight on the latter than current systems give. Employment metrics matter — students invest substantial resources in education and reasonable career outcomes are legitimate expectations. However, exclusive focus on employment metrics distorts university missions. It pushes resources toward fields with clear job pipelines while neglecting the humanities, basic sciences, and other disciplines whose value emerges over decades rather than at graduation. It encourages narrow vocational training while neglecting the broader intellectual formation that distinguishes university education from job training. Universities that produce graduates excellent at their first jobs but poorly equipped for the broader challenges of citizenship and lifelong learning serve neither students nor society as well as they could. (132 words)
questions.explanation: 【慶應型】Both/and 立場 + 既存指標の問題 + 軽視されている領域の具体列挙 + より広い formation の意義 + 結論。慶應は ビジネス文脈での価値判断 を頻出。

question_data.passage: In considerations of how human beings come to know what they know, philosophers have long distinguished between knowledge derived from individual experience and knowledge inherited through cultural transmission. The first category includes what we observe directly, what we figure out through our own reasoning, and what we test through our own actions. The second category includes everything we accept on the testimony of others — historical facts we did not witness, scientific findings we did not verify, factual claims about distant places we have never visited. The vast majority of what any individual knows falls into the second category. We are deeply dependent on the testimony of others for our basic picture of the world.

questions.stem: 下線部 'The vast majority of what any individual knows falls into the second category.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: いかなる個人にとっても、知っている事柄の圧倒的多数は、第二の範疇に属するのである。
questions.explanation: 【上智型】① The vast majority of X = X の圧倒的多数 ② what any individual knows = いかなる個人が知っていること (関係代名詞 what + any) ③ falls into the second category = 第二の範疇に属する。

question_data.passage: There is a feature of contemporary intellectual life that deserves more critical examination than it usually receives: the tendency of public discourse to operate at extremes while genuine inquiry typically lives in the middle. On contested questions, public attention concentrates on the loudest, most certain voices — those who proclaim absolute confidence in their conclusions and dismiss opposing views as foolish or malicious. Yet the most thoughtful experts on contested questions are typically far more nuanced. They acknowledge complications, identify factors that make confident conclusions difficult, and recognize that reasonable people examining the same evidence may reach different positions. The gap between thoughtful expert opinion and popular discourse versions of expert opinion has grown substantial.

questions.stem: What is the gap the author identifies?
choices: Between thoughtful expert opinion (nuanced) and popular discourse versions (polarized, certain)
questions.explanation: 段落1「The gap between thoughtful expert opinion and popular discourse versions of expert opinion has grown substantial」が根拠。

question_data.passage: The phenomenon of unintended consequences has received increasing attention in policy analysis. When governments or organizations implement specific interventions, the effects often extend beyond the targeted outcomes in ways that the original designers did not anticipate. Some unintended consequences are positive — measures intended for one purpose produce additional benefits in unrelated areas. Others are negative — interventions that solve one problem create different problems. Still others reverse the intended effects entirely, producing the opposite of what designers sought. The pattern reveals limits of human ability to predict the effects of interventions in complex systems. The implication is not that interventions should be avoided, but that they should be designed with humility about prediction limits and with mechanisms for monitoring effects and adjusting course.

questions.stem: 下線部 'they should be designed with humility about prediction limits and with mechanisms for monitoring effects and adjusting course' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 政策介入は、予測の限界に対する謙虚さと、効果を監視し方針を修正するための仕組みとともに、設計されるべきである。
questions.explanation: 【明大型】① they (=interventions) should be designed with X and with Y = X と Y とともに設計されるべき ② humility about prediction limits = 予測の限界に対する謙虚さ ③ mechanisms for monitoring effects and adjusting course = 効果を監視し方針を修正するための仕組み (動名詞並列)。

question_data.passage: Among the more striking changes of recent decades has been the dramatic increase in life expectancy in most countries. People in many wealthy nations now routinely live into their 80s and 90s, with substantial portions of populations reaching age 100. This achievement reflects improvements in nutrition, public health, medical care, and living conditions. Yet the implications of expanded longevity have only begun to be addressed. Pension and healthcare systems were designed for shorter lives. The 'three-stage life' of education, work, and retirement that organized 20th-century social arrangements works imperfectly when retirement may extend three decades. Family arrangements designed around shorter generations face new pressures when four generations may live simultaneously. The achievement of longer life requires fundamental rethinking of social arrangements that current institutions have only begun to provide.

questions.stem: 下線部 'The achievement of longer life requires fundamental rethinking of social arrangements that current institutions have only begun to provide.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: より長い生という偉業は、現行の制度が提供し始めたばかりにすぎない、社会制度の根本的な再考を要求するのである。
questions.explanation: 【青学型】① The achievement of X = X という偉業 ② longer life = より長い生 ③ requires fundamental rethinking of X = X の根本的な再考を要求する ④ social arrangements that V = V する社会制度 (関係詞節) ⑤ have only begun to V = V し始めたばかりだ。

question_data.passage: In recent years, scholars have increasingly examined the phenomenon of 'institutional decay' — the gradual deterioration of organizations that once functioned effectively. The phenomenon has been documented across many domains: governments that fail to deliver basic services they previously provided, universities that decline academically while focused on rising rankings, religious institutions that lose authority while accumulating wealth. Several mechanisms commonly contribute to institutional decay. Mission drift occurs as organizations gradually lose focus on their core purposes. Perverse incentives reward actions that damage institutional health while appearing beneficial in short-term metrics. The accumulation of organizational complexity creates opacity that masks declining performance. Talented members leave for healthier institutions, accelerating decline.

questions.stem: What mechanisms contribute to institutional decay?
choices: Mission drift, perverse incentives, organizational complexity masking decline, talent flight
questions.explanation: 段落「Mission drift ... Perverse incentives ... organizational complexity ... Talented members leave」が根拠。

question_data.passage: The relationship between economic growth and human well-being has been examined by economists for decades, with results that complicate simple stories in both directions. At one level, the connection is clear: countries that have moved from poverty to middle-income status have generally seen substantial improvements in life expectancy, education, and material conditions for ordinary citizens. At another level, the connection is weaker than growth advocates suggest: above moderate income levels, the relationship between additional economic growth and reported well-being weakens substantially. Citizens of high-income countries are not consistently happier than citizens of upper-middle-income countries. Some research suggests that beyond certain thresholds, additional economic growth produces minimal well-being gains while imposing environmental and social costs.

questions.stem: 下線部 'beyond certain thresholds, additional economic growth produces minimal well-being gains while imposing environmental and social costs' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 特定の閾値を超えると、それ以上の経済成長は環境的・社会的コストを課す一方で、福利の増加はごくわずかしか生まないのである。
questions.explanation: 【中央型】① beyond certain thresholds = 特定の閾値を超えると ② additional economic growth = それ以上の経済成長 ③ minimal well-being gains = ごくわずかな福利の増加 ④ while imposing X = X を課す一方で (分詞構文) ⑤ environmental and social costs = 環境的・社会的コスト。

question_data.passage: There is a feature of how innovations spread through societies that researchers have documented with increasing precision. Innovations rarely spread evenly. They typically spread first through specific networks — particular professions, regions, or social groups — before reaching broader populations. The pattern produces persistent inequalities in who benefits from innovations and how quickly. Medical innovations, for example, often reach educated populations and wealthy regions years before they spread to populations with weaker connections to medical research. Educational innovations spread first through schools serving advantaged families, sometimes never fully reaching schools serving disadvantaged populations. Technical innovations follow similar patterns of unequal diffusion.

questions.stem: What pattern of innovation spread does the author identify?
choices: Unequal diffusion through specific networks first, before reaching broader populations — produces persistent inequalities
questions.explanation: 段落「They typically spread first through specific networks ... The pattern produces persistent inequalities」が根拠。

question_data.passage: Among the persistent challenges of urban governance has been the management of public space — the streets, parks, plazas, and other locations that belong to no individual but to the public collectively. The challenges are multiple. Public space requires maintenance funding that no individual user has incentive to provide. It can be degraded by users whose individual benefits are small but whose collective effects are substantial. It often becomes contested between different uses — recreation versus traffic, gathering versus quiet, cultural events versus daily commerce. Decisions about public space affect quality of urban life dramatically, yet the decisions often involve tradeoffs that satisfy no group entirely.

questions.stem: 下線部 'It can be degraded by users whose individual benefits are small but whose collective effects are substantial.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 公共空間は、個々の便益は小さいが、集合的な影響は大きいような利用者たちによって、劣化させられる可能性があるのである。
questions.explanation: 【関大型】① It (公共空間) can be degraded by X = それは X によって劣化させられる可能性がある ② users whose A but whose B = A だが B であるような利用者 (関係代名詞 whose の二重) ③ individual benefits are small = 個々の便益は小さい ④ collective effects are substantial = 集合的影響は大きい (個人合理性のジレンマ)。

question_data.passage: In recent years, scholars have increasingly studied the phenomenon of 'expert humility' — the recognition by genuine experts of the limits of their own expertise. Research suggests that the most accomplished experts often display more humility about their conclusions than less accomplished practitioners in the same fields. Several factors explain this counter-intuitive pattern. Genuine expertise typically reveals the complexity of the relevant subject matter — every advance in understanding tends to surface additional complications and uncertainties. Experienced experts have more often experienced the embarrassment of being confidently wrong, learning from these experiences a more measured stance. Specialization itself reveals how much one does not know about adjacent fields.

questions.stem: 下線部 'Genuine expertise typically reveals the complexity of the relevant subject matter — every advance in understanding tends to surface additional complications and uncertainties.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 真の専門知は典型的に、当該の主題が持つ複雑さを浮き彫りにする。理解における前進はすべて、新たな複雑性と不確実性を表面化させる傾向にあるのである。
questions.explanation: 【同志社型】① Genuine expertise = 真の専門知 ② reveal the complexity of X = X の複雑さを浮き彫りにする ③ every advance in understanding = 理解におけるあらゆる前進 ④ tend to surface X = X を表面化させる傾向にある (動詞 surface = 浮き彫りにする) ⑤ additional complications and uncertainties = 新たな複雑性と不確実性。

question_data.passage: There is a paradoxical feature of contemporary information environments that deserves attention. We have access to more information than any previous generation, yet the experience of being well-informed has not become easier. The volume of information available now exceeds what any individual can attentively process. Distinguishing reliable from unreliable sources requires substantial effort that few people consistently make. Algorithmic systems shape what information we encounter in ways that often reduce rather than expand our exposure to diverse perspectives. The result is that information abundance can produce informed citizens less reliably than expected, and that thoughtful information practices have become harder rather than easier in environments of apparent abundance.

questions.stem: 下線部 'information abundance can produce informed citizens less reliably than expected' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 情報の豊富さは、期待されているほど確実には、よく情報を得た市民を生み出さないのである。
questions.explanation: 【立命館型】① information abundance = 情報の豊富さ ② produce informed citizens = よく情報を得た市民を生み出す ③ less reliably than expected = 期待されているほど確実には〜ない (less + 副詞 + than)。

question_data.passage: In medical practice, the relationship between physician and patient has undergone substantial reformulation over recent decades. The traditional model placed the physician in a position of substantial authority — diagnosing conditions, prescribing treatments, and expecting patient compliance. The contemporary model, often called 'shared decision-making,' positions the physician as advisor rather than authority. The physician brings medical expertise; the patient brings knowledge of their own values, circumstances, and preferences. Decisions emerge from collaboration rather than physician direction. The shift reflects multiple drivers. Patient autonomy has become a central value in medical ethics. Internet access has reduced information asymmetries. Multiple treatment options for many conditions make value-based choices more salient. Yet shared decision-making has its own challenges. Some patients prefer the traditional authority model. Time pressures in clinical practice limit the deeper conversations shared decision-making requires.

questions.stem: 下線部 'The physician brings medical expertise; the patient brings knowledge of their own values, circumstances, and preferences.' を日本語に訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 医師は医学的な専門知をもたらし、患者は自らの価値観、境遇、選好についての知識をもたらす。
questions.explanation: 【国公立医学部型】① The physician brings X / the patient brings Y の対比並列 ② medical expertise = 医学的専門知 ③ knowledge of their own values, circumstances, and preferences = 自らの価値観、境遇、選好についての知識 (3並列名詞)。

question_data.passage: What we call 'evidence-based medicine' has transformed clinical practice in recent decades, but it has also revealed the limits of evidence in domains where individual variation, value-laden tradeoffs, and incomplete data continue to require the practical judgment that no body of research can fully replace.

questions.stem: 上記英文を日本語に和訳しなさい。
questions.answer: 我々が「根拠に基づく医療」と呼ぶものは、近年の臨床診療を変革してきたが、同時に、個別の差異、価値判断を伴うトレードオフ、不完全なデータが、いかなる研究の蓄積によっても完全には代替し得ない実践的判断を要求し続ける領域においては、根拠そのものの限界もまた明らかにしてきたのである。
questions.explanation: 【国公立医学部型】① What we call X = 我々が X と呼ぶもの ② transform = 変革する ③ has also revealed = と同時に明らかにしてきた ④ in domains where S V = S V する領域においては ⑤ individual variation, value-laden tradeoffs, and incomplete data の3並列主語 ⑥ require the practical judgment that no body of research can fully replace = いかなる研究の蓄積によっても完全には代替し得ない実践的判断を要求する。

question_data.passage: Among the more pressing developments in contemporary medicine has been the emergence of antibiotic resistance — the evolution of bacterial strains that resist antibiotic treatment. Once-routine infections are becoming increasingly difficult to treat, with substantial implications for medical practice. Surgeries, chemotherapy, organ transplants, and other procedures depend on antibiotics to prevent and treat infection; their relative safety could be substantially eroded by widespread resistance. The causes of accelerating resistance are reasonably well understood. Overuse of antibiotics in human medicine creates selection pressure favoring resistant strains. Widespread use in livestock — both for treating illness and for promoting growth — does the same. Patient compliance issues, where treatment courses are stopped early, leave surviving bacteria that may develop resistance. International travel and trade spread resistant strains globally.

questions.stem: What causes are accelerating antibiotic resistance?
choices: Antibiotic overuse in human medicine, livestock use, patient compliance issues, international travel/trade
questions.explanation: 段落「Overuse of antibiotics in human medicine ... Widespread use in livestock ... Patient compliance issues ... International travel and trade」が根拠。

question_data.passage:
Climate change presents one of the most consequential challenges of our era. The basic science is well-established: human activities, particularly the burning of fossil fuels, are increasing greenhouse gas concentrations in the atmosphere, causing global temperatures to rise. The effects are increasingly visible — more frequent extreme weather events, rising sea levels, shifting agricultural patterns, and ecosystem disruption.

Responses to climate change involve both mitigation (reducing emissions) and adaptation (preparing for unavoidable impacts). Mitigation requires substantial transformations: shifting energy systems toward renewables, electrifying transportation, redesigning agriculture and industry to reduce emissions. Adaptation requires preparing infrastructure for sea-level rise, agricultural systems for changing growing conditions, and public health systems for new disease patterns.

The distribution of costs and benefits creates significant political dynamics. Wealthy nations have contributed most to historical emissions but face less immediate climate damage than poorer regions. Industries dependent on fossil fuels face economic transition costs. Citizens face changes to familiar patterns of life and consumption. International coordination has proven difficult under these circumstances.

questions.stem: What does mitigation require?
choices: Shifting to renewables, electrifying transportation, redesigning agriculture/industry to reduce emissions
questions.explanation: 段落2「shifting energy systems toward renewables, electrifying transportation, redesigning agriculture and industry to reduce emissions」が根拠。

question_data.passage:
The development of artificial intelligence has accelerated dramatically over the past decade. Systems that seemed like science fiction a few years ago — programs that compose music, write articles, generate images, and engage in sophisticated conversations — have become widely available tools. The implications of these capabilities are still being absorbed by societies, businesses, and individuals.

The potential benefits are substantial. AI tools can help researchers analyze vast amounts of data, doctors diagnose complex conditions, students access personalized tutoring, and businesses optimize operations. Tasks that previously required hours of human attention can be completed in seconds. Languages, technical fields, and creative pursuits become more accessible to people who previously lacked resources to engage with them.

Yet substantial concerns accompany these capabilities. AI systems can encode and amplify human biases at scale. They can be used to generate misleading content. They may displace workers in many occupations. They concentrate substantial power in a small number of organizations capable of developing the most capable systems.

questions.stem: What concerns about AI does the author identify?
choices: Encoding/amplifying biases at scale, generating misleading content, displacing workers, concentrating power
questions.explanation: 段落3「encode and amplify human biases at scale ... generate misleading content ... displace workers ... concentrate substantial power」が根拠。
